content:
Year 1347 (MCCCXLVII) was a common year starting on Monday (link will display the full calendar) of the Julian calendar, and a common year starting on Sunday (link will display the full calendar) of the Proleptic Gregorian calendar. Events. <onlyinclude Asia. Western Asia. The <a href="Mamluk%20Sultanate%20%28Cairo%29" Mamluke Empire</a is hit by the plague in the autumn. <a href="Baghdad" Baghdad</a is hit in the same year. South Asia.

After years of resistance against the Delhi Sultan <a href="Muhammad%20bin%20Tughluq" Muhammad bin Tughluq</a, the <a href="Bahmani%20Kingdom" Bahmani Kingdom</a, a Muslim Sultanate in <a href="Deccan%20Plateau" Deccan</a, was established on August 3, when King Ala-ud-din Hasan Bahman Shah was crowned in a mosque in <a href="Daulatabad%2C%20Maharashtra" Daulatabad</a.

Later in the year, the Kingdom's capital was moved from Daulatabad to the more central <a href="Gulbarga" Gulbarga</a. Southeast Asia suffered a drought which dried up an important river which ran through the capital city of the Kingdom of Ayodhya, forcing the King to move the capital to a new location on the <a href="Lop%20Buri%20River" Lop Buri River</a. Europe. Eastern and Scandinavian.

On 2 February the <a href="Byzantine%20Empire" Byzantine Empire</a's <a href="Byzantine%20civil%20war%20of%201341%E2%80%931347" civil war</a between <a href="John%20VI%20Kantakouzenos" John VI Kantakouzenos</a and the regency ended with John VI entering Constantinople.

On 8 February, an agreement was concluded with the empress <a href="Anna%20of%20Savoy" Anna of Savoy</a, whereby he and <a href="John%20V%20Palaiologos" John V Palaiologos</a would rule jointly. The agreement was finalized in May when John V married Kantakouzenos' 15-year-old daughter. The war had come at a high cost economically and territorially, and much of the Empire was in need of rebuilding.

To make matters worse, in May Genoese ships fleeing the <a href="Black%20Death" Black Death</a in Kaffa stopped in Constantinople. The plague soon spread from their ships to the city. By autumn, the epidemic had spread throughout the <a href="Balkans" Balkans</a, possibly through contact with Venetian ports along the Adriatic Sea. Specific cases were recorded in the northern Balkans on 25 December, in the city of <a href="Split%20%28city%29" Split</a.
